zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Latimer於大約1300年出生在英國時，她的父親William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby25歲，母親Lucia de Thwenge22歲。她和Ralph Basset至少有2個女兒。她去世於1348年4月30日之後在英國諾薩普頓史爾格亞特 威爾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Latimer于大约1300年出生在英国时，她的父亲William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby25岁，母亲Lucia de Thwenge22岁。她和Ralph Basset至少有2个女儿。她去世于1348年4月30日之后在英国諾薩普頓史爾格亞特 威爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Latimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a Latinist, a clerk who wrote documents in Latin, from Anglo-Norman French latinier, latim(m)ier. Latin was more or less the universal language of official documents in the Middle Ages, displaced only gradually by the vernacular – in England, by Anglo-Norman French at first, and eventually by English.

variants.name: Latner
meanings: English: occupational name for a worker in or maker of latten or brass, from Middle English latoun ‘brass’ (Old French laton). This name may sometimes have been confused with Latimer .
Altered form of German   Lattner   or   Lettner  .

variants.name: Lattner
meanings: German: occupational name for a carpenter who prepared or used laths or slats, from Middle High German latte ‘slat, lath’ + -n (plural suffix) + the agent suffix -er.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Latimer was born about 1300, in England, her father, William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby, was 25 and her mother, Lucia de Thwenge, was 22. She had at least 2 daughters with Ralph Basset. She died after 30 April 1348, in Great Weldon, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Latimer est née environ 1300, à Angleterre, son père, William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lucia de Thwenge, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 filles avec Ralph Basset. Elle est décédée après 30 avril 1348, à Great Weldon, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Latimer um 1300 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby 25 und ihre Mutter Lucia de Thwenge 22. Sie hatte mit Ralph Basset mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb nach 30. April 1348 in Great Weldon, Northamptonshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Latimer è nata il circa 1300 nel England, sua padre, William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Lucia de Thwenge, aveva 22 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figlie con Ralph Basset. Morì dopo il 30 aprile 1348, nel Great Weldon, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Latimerが約1300年にイギリスで生まれた時，父William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corbyは25歳，母Lucia de Thwengeは22歳であった。彼女はRalph Bassetとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1348年4月30日以後にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアグレアト ウエルドンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Latimer(이)가 대략 1300년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lucia de Thwenge(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 Ralph Basset(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1348년 4월 30일 이후 영국 노르섬프토실르 그리트 웰던에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Latimer nasceu aproximadamente 1300, em Inglaterra, seu pai, William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lucia de Thwenge, tinha 22 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Ralph Basset. Ela faleceu após 30 de abril de 1348, em Great Weldon, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Latimer родилась около 1300 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucia de Thwenge, 22. У неё было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Ralph Basset. Она умерла после 30 апрель 1348 г. в местности грит велдон, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Latimer nació aproximadamente 1300, en Inglaterra, su papá, William Latimer 2nd Baron Latimer of Corby, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lucia de Thwenge, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Ralph Basset. Murió después de 30 de abril de 1348, en Great Weldon, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
